m_Item1.QuestionText: Which jQuery method is used to switch between adding/removing one or more classes (for CSS) from selected elements?
m_Item1.Explanation: toggleClass() method switches between one or more classes to add/remove from selected elements.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How to read value between span tag with id dd
m_Item1.Explanation: $(“#dd”).text() used to read value between span tag with id dd

m_Item1.QuestionText: How to set value “hello world” in all div tags using jQuery?
m_Item1.Explanation: $(“div”).text(“hello world”) used to set value “hello world” in div tag .

m_Item1.QuestionText: How to set attribute title=”link” for anchor tag dynamically in jQuery
m_Item1.Explanation: $(“a”).attr(“title”,”link”) used to set attribute title=”link” for anchor tag dynamically in jQuery .

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Which of the following function of Array object returns a new array comprised of this array joined with
other arrays and/or values?
m_Item1.Explanation:
concat() function of Array object returns a new array comprised of this array joined with
other arrays and/or values .

m_Item1.QuestionText: How to read data from text box having id “tt” in jQuery?
m_Item1.Explanation: val() function allows to get the value of text field.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following jQuery method gets attributes of an element?
m_Item1.Explanation: attr() method is used to get or set the value of attribute for selected element

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the correct jQuery code to set the background color of all p elements to red?
m_Item1.Explanation: $('p').css('background-color','red');

m_Item1.QuestionText:
$('#masterList').find('li').width(500).addClass('selected');
The phenomena occuring in the above code is called?
m_Item1.Explanation: $('#masterList').find('li').width(500).addClass('selected');    is called as Chaining .

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the correct jQuery code for making all div elements 100 pixels hight?
m_Item1.Explanation: $('div').height(100)   makes all div elements height to 100px.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Which of the following jQuery method reverts the most recent 'destructive' operation, changing the set of
matched elements to its previous state?
m_Item1.Explanation:
end() method reverts the most recent 'destructive' operation, changing the set of
matched elements to its previous state .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following jQuery method gets the width property of an element?
m_Item1.Explanation: width( ) method gets the width property of an element .

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Which of the following jQuery method gets the current offset of the first matched element, in pixels,
relative to the document?
m_Item1.Explanation:
offset( ) method gets the current offset of the first matched element, in pixels,
relative to the document .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following jQuery method stops the bubbling of an event to parent elements?
m_Item1.Explanation: stopPropagation( ) method stops the bubbling of an event to parent elements .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following jQuery method checks the current selection against an expression?
m_Item1.Explanation: is(selector) method is used to check whether selected element is matching with the selector.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following jQuery method sets attributes of an element?
m_Item1.Explanation: attrname,value method sets attributes of an element.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following jQuery method apply a style on an element?
m_Item1.Explanation: addClass(classes) method apply a style on an element.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following jQuery method removes an attribute from each of the matched elements?
m_Item1.Explanation: removeAttrname() method removes an attribute from each of the matched elements.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Which of the following jQuery method returns true if the specified class is present on at least one of the set
of matched elements?
m_Item1.Explanation:
hasClassclas()  method returns true if the specified class is present on at least one of the set
of matched elements.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following jQuery method remove all or the specified classes from the set of matched elements?
m_Item1.Explanation: removeClass(class) method remove all or the specified classes from the set of matched elements.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Which of the following jQuery method adds the specified class if it is not present, remove the specified class
if it is present?
m_Item1.Explanation: toggleClass(class) method adds the specified class if it is not present,remove the specified class if it is present.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following jQuery method gets the html contents of the first matched element?
m_Item1.Explanation: html() method gets html contents innerHTML of the first matched element.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following jQuery method sets the html contents of an element?
m_Item1.Explanation: html() method sets the html contents of an element.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following jQuery method get the text contents of an element?
m_Item1.Explanation: text() method get the text contents of an element .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following jQuery method sets the text of an element?
m_Item1.Explanation: text()  method sets the text of an element .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following jQuery method get the input value of an element?
m_Item1.Explanation: val() method get the input value of an element .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following jQuery method set the value of an element?
m_Item1.Explanation: val() method set the value of an element.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following jQuery method filter out elements from a set of matched elements?
m_Item1.Explanation: filterselector()  method filter out elements from a set of matched elements .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following jQuery method reduce the set of matched elements to a single element?
m_Item1.Explanation: eqindex()  method reduce the set of matched elements to a single element .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following jQuery method selects a subset of the matchedelements?
m_Item1.Explanation: slice()  method selects a subset of the matchedelements . It needs 2 arguments start index and end index which is excluded

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Which of the following jQuery method adds more elements, matched by the given selector, to the set of
matched elements?
m_Item1.Explanation:
add(selector) method adds more elements, matched by the given selector, to the set of matched elements . e.g
$(".top").add(".middle").addClass("selected")

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Which of the following jQuery method gets a set of elements containing all of the unique immediate
children of each of the matched set of elements?
m_Item1.Explanation:
children(selector) method gets a set of elements containing all of the unique immediate
children of each of the matched set of elements .

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Which of the following jQuery method gets a set of elements containing the closest parent element that
matches the specified selector, the starting element included?
m_Item1.Explanation:
closest(selector)  method gets a set of elements containing the closest parent element that
matches the specified selector, the starting element included. parents() function will start searching element from the parent element

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Which of the following jQuery method finds all the child nodes inside the matched elements
including textnodes, or the content document, if the element is an iframe?
m_Item1.Explanation:
contentsselector() method finds all the child nodes inside the matched elements
includingtextnodes, or the content document, if the element is an iframe .

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Which of the following jQuery method searches for descendent elements that match the specified
selectors?
m_Item1.Explanation:
find(selector)  method searches for descendent elements that match the specified
selectors .

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Which of the following jQuery method gets a set of elements containing the unique next siblings of each of
the given set of elements?
m_Item1.Explanation:
next(selector) method gets a set of elements containing the unique next siblings of each of
the given set of elements .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following jQuery method finds all sibling elements after the current element?
m_Item1.Explanation: nextAll(selector) method finds all sibling elements after the current element

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Which of the following jQuery method returns a jQuery collection with the positioned parent of the first
matched element?
m_Item1.Explanation:
offsetParent(selector) method returns a jQuery collection with the positioned parent of the first
matched element .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following jQuery method gets the direct parent of an element?
m_Item1.Explanation: parent(selector) method gets the direct parent of an element .

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Which of the following jQuery method gets a set of elements containing the unique ancestors of the
matched set of elements?
m_Item1.Explanation:
parents(selector) method gets a set of elements containing the unique ancestors of the
matched set of elements .

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Which of the following jQuery method gets a set of elements containing the unique previous siblings of
each of the matched set of elements?
m_Item1.Explanation:
siblings(selector) method gets a set of elements containing the unique previous siblings of
each of the matched set of elements .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following jQuery method finds all sibling elements in front of the current element?
m_Item1.Explanation: prevAll(selector) method finds all sibling elements in front of the current element

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following jQuery method gets the style property of an element?
m_Item1.Explanation: css(name) method gets the style property of an element ..

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following jQuery method sets the style property of an element?
m_Item1.Explanation: css(name, value) method sets the style property of an element.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following jQuery method sets the height property of an element?
m_Item1.Explanation: height(value) method sets the height property of an element .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following jQuery method gets the height property of an element?
m_Item1.Explanation: height() method gets the height property of an element.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following jQuery method sets the width property of an element?
m_Item1.Explanation: width(value)   method sets the width property of an element.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following jQuery method gets the inner height excludingtheborder of an element?
m_Item1.Explanation: innerHeight() method gets the inner height excludingtheborder of an element .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following jQuery method gets the inner width excludingtheborder of an element?
m_Item1.Explanation: innerWidth () method gets the inner width excludingtheborder of an element .

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which jQuery method is used to get the direct parent of a selected element?
m_Item1.Explanation: The .parent() method in jQuery is used to retrieve the immediate parent element of each element in the matched set.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How would you remove a selected element from the DOM using jQuery?
m_Item1.Explanation: The .remove() method is used to remove the set of matched elements from the DOM entirely, along with all of their event handlers and data.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which jQuery method can be used to iterate over each element in a set of matched elements?
m_Item1.Explanation: The .each() method executes a function once for each matched element, allowing iteration over the elements in a matched set.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What jQuery method would be used to add a class to an element?
m_Item1.Explanation: The .addClass() method adds the specified class(es) to each of the set of matched elements.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which jQuery method allows you to retrieve siblings of a specified element?
m_Item1.Explanation: The .siblings() method returns all sibling elements of each element in the set of matched elements, optionally filtered by a selector.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How can you retrieve the children of an element using jQuery?
m_Item1.Explanation: The .children() method returns the direct children of each element in the set of matched elements, optionally filtered by a selector.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the purpose of the jQuery .html() method?

Options.OptionText: To change the text of an element
To change the entire HTML content of an element
To append content to the element
To insert content before the element

m_Item1.Explanation: .html() is used to get or set the HTML contents of the selected elements.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which method would you use to insert content at the end of each element in the jQuery set of matched elements?
m_Item1.Explanation: .append() inserts content at the end of the selected elements.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How would you perform a deep copy of an element and all its descendants in the DOM using jQuery?
m_Item1.Explanation: The .clone() method does a deep copy of matched elements, including all of their child nodes.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which method is used in jQuery to replace all occurrences of a selected element with new content?
m_Item1.Explanation: .replaceWith() replaces selected elements with new content.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the correct way to select an element with a data attribute 'data-role' with value 'admin' using jQuery?
m_Item1.Explanation: In jQuery, to select elements with a specific data attribute and value, you use the attribute selector: $('[data-attribute="value"]').

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which method in jQuery is used to retrieve the value of an HTML5 data attribute?
m_Item1.Explanation: The .data() method in jQuery is used to store and retrieve data associated with DOM elements. Specifically, .data('attribute') is used for retrieving HTML5 data attributes.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In jQuery Templates, what placeholder syntax is used to bind data to a template?
m_Item1.Explanation: jQuery Templates use the placeholder syntax ${data} to bind data to the template. This allows JavaScript expressions within templates.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is a primary purpose of using HTML5 data attributes?

Options.OptionText: To store custom attributes with no functionality
To store custom data private to the page or application

m_Item1.Explanation: HTML5 data attributes are used to store custom data private to the page or application. They are an easy way to add extra information to an element without using global class names or variables.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following statements is true about jQuery Templates?

Options.OptionText: jQuery Templates directly modify the DOM without compilation.
jQuery Templates were natively supported in later versions of jQuery.
jQuery Templates have been deprecated and are not recommended for use in modern web development.
jQuery Templates automatically validate user input in templates.

m_Item1.Explanation: jQuery Templates have been deprecated and are not recommended in modern web development practice. Newer templating engines like Handlebars or Mustache are suggested instead.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which jQuery method is used to get the direct parent of a specific element?
m_Item1.Explanation: The .parent() method in jQuery is used to return the direct parent element of the selected element.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What does the jQuery method .siblings() do?

Options.OptionText: Selects all children of the element
Finds all sibling elements of the selected element

m_Item1.Explanation: The .siblings() method in jQuery selects all sibling elements except for itself.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How would you select the first element in a set using jQuery?
m_Item1.Explanation: The .first() method returns the first element in the jQuery object. In this case, it selects the first element with the given class.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which method is used in jQuery to get the previous sibling of a specified element?
m_Item1.Explanation: The .prev() method in jQuery is used to find the immediate previous sibling of the selected element.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What does the .contents() method in jQuery do?

Options.OptionText: Returns the HTML content of the element
Finds all child nodes, including text and comment nodes
Replaces the content of an element
Removes the content of the element

m_Item1.Explanation: The .contents() method retrieves the child nodes (including text and comment nodes) of each element in the set of matched elements.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which jQuery method is utilized to find the closest ancestor of an element that matches a selector?
m_Item1.Explanation: The .closest() method is used to traverse the DOM up to find an element that matches the specified selector.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How do you select all elements that have no children using jQuery?
m_Item1.Explanation: The :empty selector in jQuery is used to select elements that have no children (including text nodes).

m_Item1.QuestionText: What does jQuery's .has() method do?

Options.OptionText: Sets the attribute of an element
Filters elements that contain at least one element that matches the specified selector
Checks if the element has a specific ID
Returns true if the element contains a specific text

m_Item1.Explanation: The .has() method helps filter results by providing only the elements that contain the specified element in the matched set.

m_Item1.QuestionText: To remove all children from an element in jQuery, which method can be used?
m_Item1.Explanation: The .empty() method removes all child elements and text from the selected elements, effectively clearing them.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In jQuery, what is the purpose of the .index() method?

Options.OptionText: It checks if an element exists in the DOM
It retrieves the index of the first element matching the selector
It returns the number of elements in the jQuery set
It removes an element from the jQuery set by index

m_Item1.Explanation: The .index() method returns the index position of the first occurrence of an element within its parent, compatible with a given selector.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following statements is true about jQuery plugins?

Options.OptionText: Plugins cannot be developed for jQuery.
jQuery plugins allow you to add your own methods to the jQuery library.
jQuery plugins are standalone libraries that do not base on jQuery.
Using jQuery plugins requires no knowledge of jQuery library.

m_Item1.Explanation: jQuery plugins extend the jQuery library by adding custom methods, enhancing its functionality.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which method is used to create a jQuery plugin?
m_Item1.Explanation: The $.fn.extend method is commonly used to add new functionalities to jQuery, thus creating a plugin.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the proper way to call a jQuery plugin on a set of selected elements?
m_Item1.Explanation: You call a jQuery plugin by chaining it to a jQuery object, which usually starts with a selector.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following statements is a good practice when developing a jQuery plugin?

Options.OptionText: Directly modify the global jQuery object.
Use '$' instead of 'jQuery' to keep the code shorter and faster.
Encapsulate your plugin within an immediately invoked function expression (IIFE).
Avoid using anonymous functions to improve readability.

m_Item1.Explanation: Using an IIFE helps to encapsulate the plugin code, minimizing conflicts with other components and maintaining a clean global namespace.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Why might a developer choose to use a jQuery plugin over writing custom code?

Options.OptionText: jQuery plugins are guaranteed to be faster than custom code.
Using jQuery plugins generally provides more tested and reusable code.
jQuery plugins automatically load asynchronously by default.
Custom code cannot be reused across different projects.

m_Item1.Explanation: jQuery plugins are usually well-tested and offer reusable solutions to common problems, saving time and effort in development.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
What does .find("span") do?
$("#container").find("span");
m_Item1.Explanation: .find() searches all descendant elements, regardless of depth.
